name: Port Grouped-Row Traversal Into The Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling

summary.name: Port Grouped-Row Traversal Into The Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling
summary.hypothesis: Rounds 7 and 8 on the restored grouped-rows-4 PTX surface produced the clearest saturation signal yet for the active PTX-local micro families. The export-address cleanup established a new best custom at 24.164352 ms, but only by 0.016304 ms and still as PASS_FLAT; the follow-up control-path issue-grouping tweak then regressed slightly to 24.170400 ms, again as PASS_FLAT. Across both runs the signature barely moved: active warps stayed pinned at 16.61-16.62, barrier stayed in the 5.42-5.45 band, and long-scoreboard stayed in the 7.24-7.26 band. That is enough evidence to stop spending the next round on another tiny PTX-local retime. The best next family is to preserve grouped-row locality but pivot off the active PTX microkernel onto the non-PTX 128x128 sibling, which historically stayed reasonably close to the best PTX surface while materially changing the hot-band control/export path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: The active PTX control/export surface looks saturated; the next opportunity is preserving grouped-row locality while changing the hot-band control/export implementation family away from the PTX microkernel.
summary.risk: Medium. This changes the dominant hot-band family rather than another micro-tweak, but it still stays inside the existing grouped-row 128x128 geometry instead of reopening the more disruptive 256x128 or full-band families.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164352 ms best-known PTX run
correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: The active PTX-local families have now consumed two consecutive rounds for one sub-threshold gain and one slight regression, which is enough evidence to demote them below a real family pivot.
This is the cleanest off-branch move that still preserves grouped-row locality and the 128x128 hot-band geometry instead of reopening larger geometry pivots.
It is ranked ahead of the sibling export-scratch trim because the sibling path itself should be validated before spending another round trimming its export surface.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the non-PTX sibling still trails the active PTX best by a clearly material margin without improving correctness or the stall mix enough to justify staying off the active PTX branch.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
